fits. Staff support is increasing in both quantity and quality, and leg-
islative staffers are becoming more personalized and more specialized
(Lattimer 1985). Computers and modern information systems are now
routinely part of the state legislative process.

Many of these changes or “reforms” were called for by the CCSL
study (CCSL 1971a). The explicit goal of the reforms was to encour-
age more informed, more deliberative, and more independent legisla-
tures. Legislators would now have adequate time to consider measures
before them. Salary increases would keep good people. Specialized
and personalized staffers would help legislators more competently han-
dle increasing amounts of legislation and more conscientiously service
